behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary (evaluate-stage wiring to artifact persistence key). A unit test of a helper would miss misconfigured key mapping between stage command and artifact store. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with an in-process run context and artifact store, then assert `grounding` is created/updated and `plan` is unchanged by this stage.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This checks cross-module handoff (artifact read path + implement output write path) in one flow. A unit test of read/write helpers would not catch wrong orchestration wiring. No real infrastructure or real model judgment is required to detect this regression.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage flow, and assert the produced output is written to `plan` while grounding is consumed/read without being overwritten.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: The bug is an unintended side effect in stage orchestration against shared artifact state, not isolated pure logic. Integration is needed to verify deep-dive command behavior with issue-content preconditions plus artifact persistence interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing existing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage in-process, and assert no write occurs to `plan` (or prior `plan` remains intact).

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: This is state lineage across modules/stages via shared run context. The failure appears in handoff semantics, which unit tests for individual components can miss. It does not require real OS/network/process behavior, and can be validated without asserting model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: In one in-process run context, execute evaluate-stage to write `grounding`, then execute implement-stage and verify it resolves the same lineage entry (same run context provenance) rather than missing or shadowed state.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step agent chain and verifies end-to-end effect of earlier grounding on later implementation output. This crosses multiple agentic stages in sequence; lower levels can validate plumbing but can miss chained stage-to-stage decision effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real chained run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`) in one run context and assert final `plan` content incorporates facts present in earlier `grounding` artifact from that same run.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback behavior plus artifact-state safety across components (artifact lookup + plan write + non-target artifact preservation). The real failure boundary is module interaction/state management, not external infra or model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage in a context with no `grounding` but with other pre-existing artifacts; assert `plan` is emitted with fallback logic and non-target artifacts remain unchanged.
